info.title: Pull That Up Jamie API
info.description: Public API for Pull That Up Jamie — a podcast search and research platform. Provides semantic search across podcast transcripts, corpus navigation for AI agents, research session management, and (upcoming) Lightning-based agent authentication.

tags.name: Corpus Discovery
tags.description: Read-only endpoints for navigating the podcast corpus hierarchy (feeds, episodes, chapters, topics, people). Designed for AI agents.

tags.name: Search
tags.description: Semantic search across podcast transcripts using vector embeddings.

tags.name: Research Sessions
tags.description: Create, retrieve, share, and analyze research sessions — curated collections of podcast clips.

tags.name: Create
tags.description: Generate shareable audio/video clips from podcast search results. Async processing with status polling.

tags.name: Agent Auth
tags.description: L402 Lightning-based prepaid credit system for agent API access. Hit any paid endpoint without auth to receive a 402 challenge with a Lightning invoice. After payment, use Authorization: L402 <macaroon>:<preimage> for all subsequent requests — the same credential works across all endpoints until the balance is depleted. Add ?amountSats=N to any request for a custom credit amount (min 10, max 500,000 sats). Each API call deducts its USD-equivalent cost from the prepaid balance. Compatible with lnget.

post.summary: Create audio clip with burned-in subtitles
post.description: Generates an MP4 video clip from a podcast segment with burned-in subtitles. Processing is asynchronous — poll /api/clip-status/{lookupHash} for completion. Clips are cached: identical clipId + timestamps return immediately at no extra cost. Agent pricing: $0.05/clip (50,000 microUSD). Free tier quotas: 5/week (anonymous), 10/month (registered), 50/month (subscriber). Typical processing: <1 min, max ~2 min.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: clipId (required): shareLink from a search result. timestamps (optional): [startSeconds, endSeconds] to override natural clip bounds.

404.description: Clip ID not found in corpus

get.description: Poll this endpoint to check if a clip has finished processing. No authentication required — the lookupHash serves as the credential. Recommended polling: every 5 seconds, max 30 attempts (~2.5 min timeout).

post.description: Performs vector-based semantic search across the podcast corpus using OpenAI embeddings and Pinecone. Supports filtering by feed, episode, date range, and episode name.

query.example: What is the future of Bitcoin?
What is the future of Bitcoin?
What is the future of Bitcoin?

details.example: Error message
Error message

post.description: Creates a new research session with an ordered list of Pinecone vector IDs. Requires either a Bearer JWT or a clientId for ownership.

400.description: Invalid request (missing owner or invalid pineconeIds)

details.example: pineconeIds must be a non-empty array of strings
pineconeIds must be a non-empty array of strings
Error creating research session
Error creating research session

get.summary: Get a research session by ID
get.description: Returns a specific research session with all its items and metadata. Hydrates item data from MongoDB and Pinecone as needed.

details.example: No session found for this id
No session found for this id
Error fetching research session
Error fetching research session

post.summary: Create a shareable snapshot of a session
post.description: Creates an immutable, shareable snapshot of a research session with a preview image. When nodes is omitted the backend auto-generates a 3D layout from stored session coordinates (useful for headless/agent callers).

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: nodes is optional. When omitted the server generates a layout from the session items stored coordinates.

details.example: Validation error message
Validation error message
No session found for this id and owner
No session found for this id and owner
Error sharing research session
Error sharing research session

post.summary: Analyze a research session with AI
post.description: Analyzes a research session using gpt-4o-mini and streams the response as Server-Sent Events (SSE). Optionally provide custom instructions to guide the analysis.

instructions.example: Summarize the key themes discussed across these clips

details.example: This research session has no items to analyze
This research session has no items to analyze
No session found for this id
No session found for this id
Error analyzing research session with AI
Error analyzing research session with AI

get.description: Returns metadata for a shared research session by its shareId. Intended for link unfurling and preview cards. Includes title, description, preview image, node positions, and camera configuration.

details.example: shareId path parameter is required
shareId path parameter is required
No shared research session found for this shareId
No shared research session found for this shareId
Error fetching shared research session metadata
Error fetching shared research session metadata

get.description: Returns the Corpus API specification as a markdown document. Useful for agents to understand available endpoints.
Returns aggregate counts for feeds, episodes, chapters, paragraphs, people, and topics across the entire corpus.
Returns a paginated list of all podcast feeds in the corpus.

get.summary: Get statistics for a specific feed
get.description: Returns episode, chapter, and paragraph counts plus date range for a specific feed. Useful for agents to assess feed depth before searching.

get.summary: Get a single feed by ID
get.description: Returns details for a specific podcast feed.
Returns a paginated list of episodes for a specific feed, with optional date filtering and sort order.

get.summary: Get a single episode by GUID
get.description: Returns details for a specific episode by its GUID.
Returns a paginated list of chapters for a specific episode, sorted by start time.
Returns aggregated topics derived from chapter keywords, sorted by frequency. Optionally filter by feedId.

parameters.name: feedId
parameters.description: Filter topics to a specific feed

get.description: Returns a paginated list of people (podcast creators and guests), sorted by number of appearances. Supports filtering by role, name search, and feed.

parameters.name: guestsOnly
parameters.description: Exclude hosts/creators, only show guests (default: false)

post.description: Returns a paginated list of episodes that feature a specific person (as guest or creator). Requires person name in request body.

message.example: name is required in request body
name is required in request body

get.description: Returns the remaining USD balance for the given L402 credential. Requires Authorization header with format: L402 <macaroon>:<preimage>

L402Credential.name: Authorization
L402Credential.description: L402 credential in format "L402 <base64_macaroon>:<hex_preimage>". Obtained by paying a Lightning invoice from a 402 challenge.

BearerJWT.description: JWT token for authenticated user sessions.

ClientId.name: X-Client-Id
ClientId.description: Anonymous client identifier for session tracking without authentication.
